text: I'd say I wish "sonder" was more commonly used. It's actually a real word that exists, but it deserves to be in everyday vocabulary. It describes that moment when you realize every single person you pass on the street has an inner life as complex and rich as your own—their own worries, dreams, relationships, and secrets. There's something profound about that recognition that I think more people would benefit from experiencing consciously. If we had a word that was truly household-familiar for it, maybe we'd all be a little more patient, empathetic, and humble with strangers and each other. If I had to invent something entirely new though, I'd want a word for that specific kind of contentment you feel when you're doing something small and mundane with people you care about—not a celebration or special occasion, just existing together. Like sitting quietly in the same room while everyone reads, or washing dishes together while talking. It's such a particular, good feeling, but we don't really have a concise way to name it.
